questions.question: What is a key characteristic of Random Forests in machine learning?
options: A) They use a single decision tree
B) They use multiple decision trees
C) They use a single feature
D) They use a single target variable
questions.explanation: Random Forests are an ensemble learning method that combines multiple decision trees to improve accuracy and reduce overfitting. Each tree is trained on a random subset of the data.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of bagging technique?
options: A) Boosting
B) Subagging
C) Bagging
D) Random Foresting
questions.explanation: Bagging (Bootstrap Aggregating) is a resampling technique used to reduce overfitting in machine learning models. It involves creating multiple subsets of the training data and training a model on each subset.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'stumping' term in Adaboost?
options: A) To reduce overfitting
B) To increase the number of iterations
C) To introduce noise into the model
D) To train multiple decision trees in parallel
questions.explanation: In Adaboost, 'stumping' refers to the process of training multiple decision trees in parallel, where each tree is trained on a different subset of the data. This helps to reduce overfitting and improves the overall accuracy of the model.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of Random Forests over other ensemble learning methods?
options: A) They are faster to train
B) They are more accurate than other methods
C) They can handle high-dimensional data
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Random Forests have several key advantages, including the ability to handle high-dimensional data, reduce overfitting, and improve accuracy compared to other ensemble learning methods.

questions.question: What is the main difference between Subagging and Bagging?
options: A) Subagging uses only a subset of the data
B) Subagging uses only a subset of the features
C) Bagging uses only a subset of the samples
D) Bagging uses only a subset of the features and samples
questions.explanation: Subagging is a type of bagging technique that involves creating a subset of the features and training a model on each subset. Bagging, on the other hand, involves creating a subset of the samples and training a model on each subset.
